title: lepia accedens
side_effects: Lepia accedens can have side effects if used improperly, such as inducing severe allergic reactions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Overuse of the plant can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, while prolonged exposure may cause liver damage and kidney failure. In rare cases, improper use of Lepia accedens can also cause anaphylaxis, a life-threatening allergic reaction that requires immediate medical attention. Furthermore, pregnant women and individuals with certain medical conditions may be more susceptible to these side effects.
